choices.text: 6 Keplerian elements (a, e, i, Ω, ω, ν) + Kepler's 3 laws (ellipses, equal areas, T²∝a³). Vis-viva v² = GM(2/r - 1/a) gives velocity.
2-burn elliptical transfer between circular orbits. Most fuel efficient. LEO→GEO ~3.9 km/s. Bi-elliptic better for extreme ratios. Plane changes expensive (combine w/ altitude).
Gravity assists (Voyager +10 km/s at Jupiter), aerobraking (Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter). Lagrange points stable for stationkeeping (JWST at L2 halo).
Sun-Earth L2 1.5M km opposite Sun. Single sunshade blocks Earth+Sun+Moon → cold side ~50K perfect IR. Halo orbit avoids Earth shadow. Saddle point so ~3-5 m/s/year stationkeeping.
LEO (160-2k km) cheap launch, decay; MEO (~20k km) for GPS; GEO (36k km) fixed view comm; SSO ~98° for imaging consistency; polar for full coverage. Match orbit to mission requirements.
